steps.title: Identify what the researcher's claim requires
steps.content: The researcher claims that some lakes have seen an increase in ice duration, contradicting the general declining trend from 1980 to 2006. To support this claim, the data must show at least one lake with more ice days in 2005–06 than in 1980–81—a direct comparison over time.

steps.title: Match the lake showing an increase to the effective evidence
steps.content:
Reading the graph: Lake Baikal increased from approximately 100 days in 1980–81 to approximately 110–120 days in 2005–06. Oulujarvi declined (200 to 160 days) and Kegonsa remained flat (110 to 110 days). Choices A and B state values from only one winter without proving an increase; choice D incorrectly claims two lakes increased when Kegonsa and Oulujarvi either stayed the same or declined. Choice C directly proves an increase by comparing Baikal's ice duration across both periods, providing exactly the evidence the researcher needs.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
